    Move the contents of file at *src* to path-or-filelike *dst*.

_move_path_to_path_or_streamg
    Subset *chars* from the font at *font_path* into a Type 3 font.
    Parameters
    ----------
    font_path : path-like
        Path to the font to be subsetted.
    chars : str
        The characters to include in the subsetted font.
    Returns
    -------
    str
        The string representation of a Type 3 font, which can be included
        verbatim into a PostScript file.
    r
hinting_factorc
get_char_index

z%_font_to_ps_type3.<locals>.<listcomp>aM
%!PS-Adobe-3.0 Resource-Font
%%Creator: Converted from TrueType to Type 3 by Matplotlib.
10 dict begin
/FontName /{font_name} def
/PaintType 0 def
/FontMatrix [{inv_units_per_em} 0 0 {inv_units_per_em} 0 0] def
/FontBBox [{bbox}] def
/FontType 3 def
/Encoding [{encoding}] def
/CharStrings {num_glyphs} dict dup begin
/.notdef 0 def
/{}N)
format

z$_font_to_ps_type3.<locals>.<genexpr>)
Z	font_nameZ

bboxrk
num_glyphsz
end readonly def
/BuildGlyph {
 exch begin
 CharStrings exch
 2 copy known not {pop /.notdef} if
 true 3 1 roll get exec
 end
} _d
/BuildChar {
 1 index /Encoding get exch get
 1 index /BuildGlyph get exec
} _d
FontName currentdict end definefont pop

_font_to_ps_type3w
    Subset *chars* from the font at *font_path* into a Type 42 font at *fh*.
    Parameters
    ----------
    font_path : path-like
        Path to the font to be subsetted.
    chars : str
        The characters to include in the subsetted font.
    fh : file-like
        Where to write the font.

mtextc
The PS backend determines usetex status solely based on rcParams['text.usetex'] and does not support having usetex=True only for some elements; this element will thus be rendered as if usetex=False.F

z!RendererPS.draw_gouraud_trianglesr
        Emit the PostScript snippet *ps* with all the attributes from *gc*
        applied.  *ps* must consist of PostScript commands to construct a path.
        The *fill* and/or *stroke* kwargs can be set to False if the *ps*
        string already includes filling and/or stroking, in which case
        `_draw_ps` is just supplying properties and clipping.
        r
fill
z	%f %f %f z
%s setpattern fill grestore
stroke

svt"
        Render the figure to a filesystem path or a file-like object.
        Parameters are as for `.print_figure`, except that *dsc_comments* is a
        all string containing Document Structuring Convention comments,
        generated from the *metadata* parameter to `.print_figure`.
        rD
z,outfile must be a path or a file-like objectrK

FigureCanvasPS._print_figurec
        If :rc:`text.usetex` is True, a temporary pair of tex/eps files
        are created to allow tex to manage the text layout via the PSFrags
        package. These files are processed to yield the final ps or eps file.
        The rest of the behavior is as for `._print_figure`.
        rD
%!PS-Adobe-3.0 EPSF-3.0
%%EndComments
%%BeginProlog
/mpldict z
 dict def
mpldict begin
%%EndProlog
mpldict begin
 0 0 clipbox

rotatedrr

Z	fixed_dpiZ	filetypesrE
delete_parameterr^
partialmethodZ
print_psZ	print_epsru
psfragsZ
font_preambleZ
custom_preambler{
convert_psfragsQ
    When we want to use the LaTeX backend with postscript, we write PSFrag tags
    to a temporary postscript file, each one marking a position for LaTeX to
    render some text. convert_psfrags generates a LaTeX document containing the
    commands to convert those tags to text. LaTeX/dvips produces the postscript
    file that includes the actual text.
    z
text.latex.preambler
graphicxr
z:\geometry{papersize={%(width)sin,%(height)sin},margin=0in})
\begin{figure}
  \centering\leavevmode
  %(psfrags)s
  \includegraphics*[angle=%(angle)s]{%(epsfile)s}
\end{figure}r
epsfile
dvipsz
-R0z
-oZ	Landscapei

%s.  Distillation step skipped.)

funcr
excr 
    Use ghostscript's pswrite or epswrite device to distill a file.
    This yields smaller files without illegal encapsulated postscript
    operators. The output is low-level, converting text to outlines.
    
	-dEPSCropz
-sPAPERSIZE=%sz
.psz
ps.distiller.res
-dBATCHz	-dNOPAUSEz
-r%dz
-sDEVICE=ps2writez
-sOutputFile=%s
_get_executable_info
executabler
removern
pstoeps)
paper_optionr
    Use ghostscript's ps2pdf and xpdf's/poppler's pdftops to distill a file.
    This yields smaller files without illegal encapsulated postscript
    operators. This distiller is preferred, generating high-level postscript
    output that treats text as text.
    r
pdftopsz
tmp.pdfro
ps2pdfz

-sGrayImageFilter#FlateEncodez
-sColorImageFilter#FlateEncoder
-sPAPERSIZE#%sz
-paper
matchz

tmppsr 
    Return a postscript header string for the given bbox lbrt=(l, b, r, t).
    Optionally, return rotate command.
    z
%.2f %.2f translate
90 rotater
%%%%BoundingBox: %d %d %d %dz)%%%%HiResBoundingBox: %.6f %.6f %.6f %.6fr

rotate
	bbox_infoZ
hires_bbox_infor 
]|}	|	
qH|	
],}	|	
    Convert the postscript to encapsulated postscript.  The bbox of
    the eps file will be replaced with the given *bbox* argument. If
    None, original bbox will be used.
    r
.epsr

%!PS-Adobe-3.0 EPSF-3.0
%%EndCommentssp
